name: Streamlit
tagline: Turn Python scripts into interactive data and ML web apps
seoTitle: Streamlit - Build Data Apps in Python
metaDescription: Streamlit is an open-source Python framework that turns scripts into interactive data, ML, and chat web apps with simple widget calls and no frontend code.
overview: Streamlit is a Python framework for building interactive web apps from plain scripts. You write normal Python and call simple functions like st.slider or st.write, and Streamlit renders the widgets and output as a web page. There is no separate HTML, CSS, or JavaScript to manage.

It is aimed at data scientists, ML engineers, and Python developers who want to share dashboards, reports, or models without building a frontend. As you edit your script, the app updates so you can prototype and gather feedback quickly.

As a data app builder, Streamlit fits the gap between a notebook and a full web stack. You keep your work in one Python file, and you can deploy and share it through the free Streamlit Community Cloud.
features: Build interactive apps in pure Python, with no frontend code required
Input widgets such as sliders, plus dataframes, charts, and layout helpers
Live editing: the app updates as you change and save your script
Multi-page apps for organizing larger projects
Extra functionality through community-built Streamlit Components
Free deployment and sharing via Streamlit Community Cloud

gettingStarted.intro: Install Streamlit with pip, confirm it works with the built-in demo, then write and run your first app.

steps.heading: Install and verify
steps.body: Install Streamlit from PyPI, then run the bundled Hello app to confirm your setup. It should open in your browser.

steps.heading: Write a minimal app
steps.body: Create a file named streamlit_app.py with a slider and some output.
steps.code:
import streamlit as st
x = st.slider("Select a value")
st.write(x, "squared is", x * x)

steps.heading: Run your app
steps.body: Start the app from your terminal; it opens in the browser and reloads as you edit the script.

useCases: Build an interactive dashboard to explore a dataset and share it with your team
Wrap a machine learning model in a simple UI so others can try it with their own inputs
Create an LLM or chatbot app where users type prompts and see responses
Generate live reports that update as the underlying data or parameters change
